concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-001 (AUROC ≥ 0.70 and AUPRC ≥ 0.65) is only indirectly referenced by integration test T026; there is no explicit task that validates the numeric thresholds against the specification, leaving the criterion unverified.

Success Criterion SC-002 (at least one GO term with adjusted p < 0.05) is only checked by integration test T030, but no dedicated task ensures the enrichment script enforces the FDR threshold during normal pipeline runs.

Success Criterion SC-003 (total wall‑clock time ≤ 6 h) is addressed by benchmark task T037, yet T037 only logs runtime; there is no task that aborts the pipeline if the 6‑hour limit is exceeded, so the criterion is not enforceable.

Success Criterion SC-004 (reproducibility with identical seed) is partially covered by CI task T038, but no explicit task verifies that all stochastic modules (e.g., random‑graph baseline, any tie‑breaking in correlation) actually consume the global `--seed` flag.

Success Criterion SC-005 (presence and parsability of all required output files) lacks a dedicated validation task; while T011 validates contracts, there is no task that explicitly checks for the existence of every required file after each pipeline target.

The Quickstart documentation (quickstart.md) is referenced in the plan but no task in tasks.md is assigned to create, verify, or test the quickstart steps, resulting in an orphan documentation element.
The specification mandates aborting a GEO series with fewer than 20 samples, yet no task implements or tests this abort behavior.

concerns.location: spec.md:Edge‑case handling – missing or malformed STRING file
concerns.text: The specification requires a clear error when the STRING reference is missing or unreadable; no task is currently defined to detect, report, or test this condition.
